repos_analyzed.name: forcedotcom/code-analyzer
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: Salesforce static analysis, rule selection, severity thresholds, machine-readable outputs, workspace versus target behavior

repos_analyzed.name: trailheadapps/lwc-recipes
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: LWC implementation patterns, Salesforce DX metadata structure, LWC Jest usage, UI API, navigation, events, forms, GraphQL, static resources

repos_analyzed.name: trailheadapps/apex-recipes
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: Apex patterns for security, SOQL, DML, async, triggers, files, callouts, custom metadata, platform events, testing, and Salesforce DX structure

repos_analyzed.name: salesforce/sfdx-lwc-jest
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: LWC Jest setup, scripts, CLI options, coverage, debug mode, wire testing, and project-specific Jest overrides

repos_analyzed.name: dangmai/prettier-plugin-apex
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: Optional Apex formatting, check-first validation, debug-check behavior, parser options, scoped formatting guidance

repos_analyzed.name: salesforce/eslint-plugin-lwc
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: LWC-specific ESLint rules for decorators, wire usage, DOM access, async behavior, event listener cleanup, template safety, and SSR concerns

repos_analyzed.name: salesforce/eslint-config-lwc
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: Shareable LWC ESLint config profiles, ESLint version compatibility, base/recommended/extended/i18n/SSR/TypeScript config selection

repos_analyzed.name: salesforce/eslint-plugin-lwc-mobile
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: Mobile/offline LWC lint rules, GraphQL limits, Apex import review, and mobile-safe component validation

repos_analyzed.name: salesforce/eslint-plugin-lwc-graph-analyzer
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: Advanced LWC static analyzability, data priming and offline readiness, wire dependency graph checks, template inclusion requirements

repos_analyzed.name: forcedotcom/salesforcedx-vscode
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: Salesforce VS Code workflow, language service coverage, layered quality scripts, link checks, duplicate checks, dependency checks, secret scan concepts

repos_analyzed.name: mitchspano/sfdx-scan-pull-request
repos_analyzed.primary_intelligence: Pull request scan automation concepts, changed-scope scanning, inline comments, check-run reporting, severity thresholds, legacy scanner options

p0_actions: Create or strengthen a Salesforce Code Analyzer command matrix with workspace, target, rule selector, severity threshold, view, output file, pass, warning, fail, and skipped interpretation.
Create or strengthen LWC Jest guidance covering package scripts, CLI options, coverage, debug mode, project mocks, wire adapter tests, and evidence rules.
Create or strengthen LWC ESLint guidance mapping key LWC rules to common Codex mistakes.
Create or strengthen mobile/offline LWC lint and graph analyzer guidance with clear applicability rules.
Create or strengthen Apex formatting guidance that defaults to check-first and avoids broad unrelated formatting.
Create or strengthen external recipe usage rules so Codex treats Apex Recipes and LWC Recipes as pattern references, not copy sources.
Update task prompts to require available quality gates after relevant Salesforce code changes and to document skipped gates honestly.
Update checklists so Codex records validation evidence, skipped tool reasons, and Memory/History updates.
p1_actions: Add or refine CI workflow strategy for Markdown checks, public safety checks, Salesforce Code Analyzer, LWC lint/Jest, and Apex formatting.
Add Salesforce project setup-flow guardrails that distinguish sample app setup commands from user project commands.
Add VS Code developer workflow guidance for optional Salesforce Extension Pack, Code Analyzer extension, language services, local quality checks, and safe org-aware work.
Add pull request review automation strategy using severity thresholds, changed-file scope, and check-run style reporting.
Add ESLint config selection guidance for base, recommended, extended, i18n, SSR, TypeScript, ESLint 9, and older project compatibility.
Enhance automation scripts to detect available tools and package scripts before recommending commands.
Add example-only package.json scripts and config snippets for users to adapt to their real Salesforce DX projects.
Strengthen vendor reference attribution with repo URLs, license identities, no-vendoring boundaries, and optional clone guidance.
p2_actions: Add advanced SARIF and JSON analyzer reporting examples for CI.
Add mobile/offline graph analyzer decision tree for Salesforce mobile and offline-first LWC work.
Add Apex Recipes and LWC Recipes task playbooks that route Codex to relevant concepts by task type.
Add optional changed-file pull request scan strategy after validating current Code Analyzer support.
Add duplicate, dependency, link-check, and secret-scan repo hygiene guidance inspired by Salesforce VS Code workflows.
Add optional TypeScript LWC guidance after checking current Salesforce DX support and user project setup.
commands_to_document: sf code-analyzer run --rule-selector Recommended --workspace . --target force-app/main/default --view table
risks: External tool commands and package compatibility can change; verify against installed CLI help or upstream docs before executing.
Copying sample code or metadata from recipe repos can introduce wrong objects, fields, permissions, or data assumptions.
Broad Apex formatting can produce noisy diffs and hide functional changes.
Recipe setup commands can create orgs, deploy metadata, assign permissions, or import data and must not be run without explicit user approval.
Legacy scanner action guidance may be outdated compared to Salesforce Code Analyzer.
ESLint config recommendations can conflict with older project ESLint versions.
Mobile/offline linting may not apply to desktop-only LWC projects.
Analyzer outputs should not be written into Salesforce metadata folders unless explicitly intended.
External source, generated artifacts, node_modules, .git folders, or sample apps must not be vendored into the public repo.
Attribution and license notes must stay current when new external intelligence is added.
recommended_next_prompt: Using WORKSPACE/EXTERNAL_REPO_INTELLIGENCE_STRATEGY.md and WORKSPACE/EXTERNAL_REPO_INTELLIGENCE_STRATEGY.json, update the repo's TOOLS, QUALITY_GATES, AUTOMATION, VENDOR_REFERENCES, SALESFORCE_KNOWLEDGE reference maps, Codex prompt pack, and Codex checklist pack with original public-safe guidance. Do not copy external source code, do not vendor repos, preserve attribution by URL, run public safety and link/path checks, and do not commit or push until approved.
